seoTitle: How to Activate Gmail in Squarespace with Workspace
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Google Workspace to Squarespace, update MX records, and activate Gmail for a professional email address on your domain.
intro: Running professional email on your custom domain means connecting Google Workspace to your Squarespace site and getting the DNS settings exactly right. This guide covers setting up a Google Workspace account, connecting your domain in Squarespace, updating MX records, and finally activating Gmail. It's aimed at small business owners, freelancers, and personal brands who want a working business email address under their own domain.

steps.heading: Set up your Google Workspace account
steps.body: Sign up for Google Workspace and choose the plan that fits your business email needs.

steps.heading: Connect your domain in Squarespace settings
steps.body: In your Squarespace account, go to the domain settings and start the process to connect it with Google Workspace.

steps.heading: Update MX records and verify DNS
steps.body: Add the MX records Google Workspace provides into Squarespace's DNS settings, and complete any domain verification steps.

steps.heading: Activate Gmail inside Workspace
steps.body: Once verification and MX records are confirmed, activate the Gmail app within your Google Workspace account.

steps.heading: Test your email setup
steps.body: Send and receive a test email to confirm everything under your custom domain is working properly.

faq.question: How long does it take for Gmail to work after connecting Squarespace and Google Workspace?
faq.answer: It depends on DNS propagation, which can take anywhere from a few minutes to a day after the MX records are updated.

faq.question: What if I get an MX record error while connecting Squarespace to Google Workspace?
faq.answer: Double-check that the MX records exactly match what Google Workspace provided, since even small typos will cause verification or delivery failures.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Squarespace plan to connect Google Workspace?
faq.answer: You need a Squarespace plan that includes domain management access, since that's where the DNS changes for Google Workspace are made.

faq.question: Can I still use my old email while switching to Google Workspace?
faq.answer: Your old email may keep working until the MX record change fully propagates, after which mail will route through Google Workspace instead.
